calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica for and with Frank Alaimo.

Ms. Zulaica stated that they have submitted everything and addressed the examiner's notes, gave options on Carl's share stating that they are not sure how to proceed, that extraordinary fees were requested for both the attorney and executor, that there were over 17 beneficiaries, that there were multiple tax years in arrears, that there were multiple trips to the IRS, that there were liens to be dealt with, that there were tenant issues, that repairs had to be made, that there were issues regarding Mr. Salma making it difficult to do the inventory, that they had to research the elder abuse claims, that there were numerous meetings, that there was a petition to exercise option under the will and that they had issues with the optionee and had to get court approval, that they had to file an ex parte petition to hire a landlord tenant attorney, that Ms. Zulaica is the second attorney in this case and that Mr. Alaimo has spent over 400 hours in this estate taking time out from his small business. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Continued To Feb-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Jan-04-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Personal Representative To Order To Convey Property To Optionee Pursant To Testamentary Option And Etc.
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica for and with (Executor/Beneficiary) Frank Alaimo. Mark Romeo for (optionee) David Salma. Yvonne Alaimo Rosko & Anna-Marie Alaimo Cline (beneficiaries) also present.

Mr. Romeo stated that all of his argument is in the papers, that they are trying to get instructions as to the assignability to an LLC and that they have responded fully on the arguments on the option. Ms. Zulaica stated that the executor petitioned to close the sale at $1,300,000.00, that issues arose regarding the language in the will regarding the purchase, that the person buying the property has to be on the title, that Mr. Salma can do what he wants after the purchase, that the will states sale to David Salma only and that the LLC is a different entity, that they are trying to honor the terms of the will and that if he is unable to purchase the home it will revert to the estate. The Court asked Mr. Romeo why Mr. Salma wants the title as an LLC. Mr. Romeo stated that an LLC is a common way of holding property, that it is easier to obtain financing and that the contract has not been signed and can be changed as to however the court rules. Ms. Zulaica stated that the request is for Mr. Salma to purchase as an individual and then transfer, that the property is worth more than 1.3 million but they are abiding by the will and that if he cannot abide by the will why should the estate suffer. Ms. Cline stated that her aunt wrote the holographic will before surgery, that she intended for Mr. Salma to benefit from the property, that the 1.3 million was fair market value in 2003, that she also wanted the family to benefit, that she does not feel he can perform as an individual and feels that he is just looking to make a profit. Mr. Romeo stated that there are no strings attached to the option, that they want to proceed, that they have done everything that has been asked of them to move the matter forward, that this should have closed in January and that they are entitled to the instruction asked for. Ms. Zulaica stated that they have also been working to move forward and that Mr. Salma could have petitioned earlier. Ms. Cline further stated that the will was written 10 years ago, that her aunt was solvent at that time, that subsequent to 2003 her aunt was asked if she still wanted Mr. Salma to have the property and she said no, that Mr. Salma changed the locks on the property immediately after Ms. Alaimo's death, that the family is unable to go in and find her papers and that they have concerns about not being able to find her calendars for the last 4 years. The petition by personal representative to order to convey property to optionee was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
